content.assessment: This baseline preserves the current institutional architecture unchanged: tankerületi centers retain sole formal authority over closure decisions (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01), applying enrollment/efficiency criteria (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03) under fiscal pressure from national funding formulas (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04). Because no new rule-based procedure is introduced, the existing pattern whereby closure outcomes reflect political/administrative discretion rather than demographic inevitability (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02) continues, and the 38% settlement coverage gap (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06) is left to evolve via the same mechanisms that produced it.
opportunities: Could still benefit from the planned 2026 tankerület restructuring, which will adjust principal/municipal input even without a new statutory framework for demographic decline (F-live-hungarian_education_system-07)
strengths: Requires no institutional redesign, so administrative compatibility with existing tankerület governance is total (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01)

Consistent with historical precedent that closure/survival patterns are policy-driven rather than fixed, leaving open the possibility of future non-statutory correction (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02)
threats: Continued reliance on centralized discretionary closure criteria (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03) risks further uneven sorting of villages, compounding rather than resolving the coverage gap (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06)

Kindergarten-level access gaps governed by the same enrollment-threshold logic (F-live-hungarian_education_system-08) will likely continue to compound primary-level attrition under a no-change trajectory
weaknesses: Leaves the village-level authority gap over closure decisions completely unaddressed (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01)
Does nothing to relieve the structural fiscal pressure from national class-size/per-pupil formulas that operates independently of any governance reform (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04)
Perpetuates the territorial coverage gap already affecting 38% of settlements without any corrective mechanism (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06)
